proof_limits: compose_building assembles caller / COO declared node and edge choices only

axis_responsibility.Agent: Owns performer identity/capability per node (dev/qa-lead/inspector/cto-lead/coo). adapter_ref is a brain connector only; capability (read vs write) is bounded by adapter capability ∩ tool-policy ∩ Brick write_scope NEED. Gemini lane identity is constrained to inspector/qa by adapter admission.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Owns the new dogfood preset (work contracts, per-node return shapes, graph_topology composition). The preset DECLARES nodes/edges/fan-in; it does not run them.
axis_responsibility.Link: Owns all Movement (forward fan-out, reroute on implementation_gap), fan-in-wait-all frontier ordering, and gate sufficiency. closure transition target + reroute budget are human/Link-declared, never support-synthesized.
axis_responsibility.support: Records/walks/projects the plan and evidence only; no source truth, success, quality, or Movement.

candidate_file_changes: PROPOSED (not written): brick/templates/presets/four-llm-standard-graph.md — new dogfood preset (catalog_scope dogfood/brick-protocol) modeled on brick/templates/presets/triage-fanout-3.md + review-fleet.md, declaring the 5-node work-codex -> {claude,gemini,fugu lenses} -> closure graph_topology with per-step selected_adapter_ref/selected_model_ref and gate_concept_profile [strict-evidence, fan-in-wait-all].

PROPOSED (optional, smaller smoke variant): brick/templates/presets/four-llm-readonly-smoke.md — same 4-provider fan-in board with NO write node (all read-only inspect lenses) for the preflight connect smoke.
NO change proposed to agent/objects/* (inspector already admits gemini), adapter_constants.py, or link/*.yaml.
Next Building: a COO-declared run Building referencing the new preset (or the smoke preset first) with a narrow declared write_scope on the work node only.
checker_or_verifier_plan: Reuse existing preset/topology validation: the materializer that emits declared graph_topology groups+edges (proven green for triage-fanout-3, two-fan-in-graph) validates the new preset's fan_out/fan_in/terminal.
Axis-owned-field scan: ensure edge/group endpoints use from/to/converge_on handles (never bare Link-owned 'target') so the Brick-template axis scan stays green.
Agent binding admission check: assert each node's selected_adapter_ref ∈ its agent_object adapter_refs (catches the Gemini-lane-on-wrong-object case).
write_scope intersection check: assert only work-codex declares write_scope NEED and that its adapter is an observed-write adapter; lenses carry none.
Gate resolution check: link-gate refs resolvable; fan-in-wait-all frontier wired to converge_on closure (gatefact_literal:none, frontier observation present).
Read-only smoke as preflight verifier: per-provider one-file read returns a valid return shape before any write run.
Avoid --all; run only the targeted preset/topology + binding checkers.
returned.design_summary: Smallest standard 4-LLM dogfood graph = a single fan-out/fan-in board structurally identical to the existing triage-fanout-3 / review-fleet pattern, but with one provider per lane: Codex implementation root fans out to three read-only review lenses (Claude structure/QA, Gemini broad/alt-sanity, Fugu axis/boundary attack) that fan in to one COO closure synthesis. Core = 5 nodes (work + 3 lenses + closure). No new engine mechanism is needed; the graph is expressible as a new dogfood preset declaring graph_topology. The only non-trivial axis constraint discovered: the Gemini lane is admissible ONLY through agent-object:inspector (or qa for gemini-local), because no leader/dev/qa-lead/cto-lead object admits a gemini adapter. HOLD-safety comes for free from the existing closure_transition_target_policy (implementation_gap -> budgeted reroute to the work root; verification_gap -> hold) plus gate_concept_profile [strict-evidence, fan-in-wait-all]. Everything here is structurally composable now; live 4-provider execution remains not_proven.
edge_cases: Gemini adapter not admitted by the intended leader object (cto-lead/qa-lead/dev/coo) — binding fails; must route Gemini lane through inspector/qa.

One provider unavailable at run (no gemini key, sakana routing down, codex/claude CLI missing) — fan-in-wait-all should expose a held/missing source frontier, NOT silently close; record provider absence in not_proven, not as a transition_concern.
Partial fan-in (2 of 3 lenses return) — closure must surface the missing source rather than synthesize over a hole.
work-codex write_scope declared as a bare directory ('dir/') instead of 'dir/*' — children excluded (known write_scope glob trap); preflight should fnmatch-probe the scope.
Reroute storm on repeated implementation_gap — bounded by node_reroute_budget work:1; verification_gap holds instead of looping.
transition_concern related_boundary_refs aimed at self or a building-boundary sentinel — engine silently walks on; concerns for real defects must target the upstream work node (brick:<work-node-id>).
claude-local runtime write remains unproven (P3 carryover) — if work-codex were ever swapped to claude-local, runtime write is not yet demonstrated.
invariants: Exactly one write node (work-codex) carries a write_scope NEED; the three lenses and closure are read-only.

Effective write opens only at the intersection: Brick write_scope NEED + agent tool-policy:read-write-scoped + observed-write adapter (codex-local). Gemini can never be the write lane (not an observed-write adapter).
Gemini lane node MUST bind an agent_object whose adapter_refs admit the gemini adapter (inspector for gemini-api/local, qa for gemini-local) — else binding is invalid.
All movement literals are forward|reroute only; no other movement value.
fan-in-wait-all must wait for all three lenses or expose a held/missing frontier; the Building must never appear complete if a lens did not join.
transition_concern_evidence from the lenses/closure is non-binding and never selects Movement or target; it may only inform a later Link/human disposition.
reroute budget / closure routing target are constitutional-default or preset-default, never chosen by support at runtime.
not_proven: Live 4-provider execution — no provider was run (read-only design only).
Provider availability: gemini-local CLI / gemini-api key, codex-fugu-local sakana routing reachability, codex-local and claude-local runtime presence at run time.
Runtime parallel execution — existing presets explicitly state 'preset is not runtime parallel execution'; actual concurrent fan-out behavior is unobserved.
Claude-local runtime write proof (P3 carryover) — structurally write-capable, runtime write still unproven.
model:sakana:fugu / model:gemini:default actually resolve to a reachable backend at run.
Semantic fitness of the proposed lane->provider mapping and whether one fan-in closure is sufficient for hard QA (a second mid-closure / two-fan-in-graph Y-shape may be needed).
Future Building run correctness and future Agent/provider quality.
Whether the new preset materializes green — not run (would be verified by the topology/binding checkers above).

TOOL-ACCESS NOTE: initial Read/Grep attempts under /Users/smith/projects/BRICK were permission-denied because the active cwd is the worktree /Users/smith/.brick/worktrees/struct-surgery-0623; all evidence_refs were read from that worktree root only.
observed_evidence: Read brick/templates/bricks/design/return.yaml: design return shape = the 13 fields I am filling; carries_forward_fields = proposed_changes,reading_scope_map,invariants,axis_responsibility,candidate_file_changes; forbidden_return_keys include success/failure/approved/quality/movement_choice/route_target.

Read project/brick-protocol/buildings/four-llm-standard-graph-dogfood-design-0625/work/declared-building-plan.json: this Building is a 3-node linear design->review->closure chain (preset building-chain-preset:design-contract-only, plan_shape graph, declared_by coo-codex); design node = adapter:claude-local/model:claude:inherit/agent-object:design-lead; all edges movement=forward with declared_gate_refs=[link-gate:default-transition]; closure edge closes the boundary. The 4-LLM graph being DESIGNED is the SUBJECT, not this Building's own topology.

Read support/connection/adapter_constants.py: adapter capability table — adapter:codex-local {read,write}; adapter:codex-fugu-local {read,write} (provider routing sakana, same codex exe); adapter:claude-local {read,write,web}; adapter:gemini-local {read,review,web}; adapter:gemini-api {read,review}. Model refs: model:codex:default, model:claude:inherit, model:gemini:default/flash, model:sakana:fugu / fugu-ultra. _OBSERVED_WRITE_ADAPTER_REFS = {codex-local, codex-fugu-local, claude-local} (gemini is NOT a write adapter).

Read agent objects: dev(worker, prefers codex-local, tool-policy read-write-scoped), qa-lead(leader, prefers claude-local), coo(leader, claude), cto-lead(leader, claude), inspector(reviewer), qa(reviewer). Only agent-object:inspector admits BOTH adapter:gemini-local AND adapter:gemini-api; agent-object:qa admits gemini-local only; dev/qa-lead/cto-lead/coo do NOT admit any gemini adapter (Grep agent/objects gemini -> inspector.yaml + qa.yaml only). All six admit adapter:codex-fugu-local.

Read brick/templates/presets/triage-fanout-3.md and two-fan-in-graph.md and design-build-parallel.md and review-fleet.md: the fan-out/fan-in mechanism already exists — graph_topology{edges, fan_out_groups, fan_in_groups{converge_on, sources, closure_transition_target_policy{implementation_gap:{action:target,target_step_template_ref}, verification_gap:{action:hold}}}, terminal}, node_reroute_budgets, gate_concept_profile:[strict-evidence, fan-in-wait-all]. review-fleet is the existing mixed-provider exemplar (one lens carries selected_model_ref model:claude:sonnet) and explicitly states 'preset is not runtime parallel execution'.

Read link/movement.yaml, link/transition.yaml, link/carry.yaml, link/gate.yaml: Movement literals are ONLY {forward, reroute}; GateFact stages {transfer,carry,movement}, sufficiency {sufficient,insufficient,missing_required_facts}; fan-in-wait-all has gatefact_literal:none (frontier/observation that must_not 'make Building look complete when join did not run'); transition_concern_evidence may inform later Link disposition but does NOT choose Movement; route target / reroute budget are HUMAN/Link decisions, never support-synthesized.

Read brick/templates/shapes/catalog.yaml: presets live under brick/templates/presets/; common vs dogfood split by per-preset catalog_scope; gate tokens translate to link-gate:* refs at materialization.

core_4llm_graph_nodes.return_shape_intent: brick/templates/bricks/work/return.yaml (produces artifact + evidence_refs the 3 lenses read)
core_4llm_graph_nodes.write_scope_need: YES — a narrow declared write_scope NEED (e.g. a single scoped target dir/file with glob form 'dir/*'); this is the ONLY write node

core_4llm_graph_nodes.return_shape_intent: brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/return.yaml or review/return.yaml — structure read / QA / cross-check

core_4llm_graph_nodes.agent_object_ref: agent-object:inspector (REQUIRED — only object admitting a gemini adapter; or agent-object:qa for gemini-local)
core_4llm_graph_nodes.return_shape_intent: brick/templates/bricks/review/return.yaml or evidence-integrity/return.yaml — broad / alternative sanity
core_4llm_graph_nodes.write_scope_need: NONE (read/review only; gemini is not a write adapter)
NONE (write-capable adapter but used read-only here)

core_4llm_graph_nodes.return_shape_intent: brick/templates/bricks/closure/return.yaml; surfaces non-binding transition_concern_evidence for hard fan-in QA

proposed_changes.evidence_ref_handoff_expectations: work-codex must emit evidence_refs (file paths it wrote/inspected) in its return; each lens must include evidence_used/evidence_refs grounded in inspected artifacts; closure carries narrowly_proven + remaining_delta + transition_concern_evidence forward. Carry envelope (observed_evidence/not_proven/evidence_reference) stays per CarryFact closed shape.

link_movement_and_fanin_policy.closure_transition_target_policy: implementation_gap -> reroute back to work-codex root (budgeted node_reroute_budget work:1); verification_gap -> hold (waits for human/COO disposition) = HOLD-safe
link_movement_and_fanin_policy.edge_movement: all forward; gate per edge declared_gate_refs default link-gate:default-transition
link_movement_and_fanin_policy.fan_in_ordering: fan-in-wait-all frontier waits for ALL three lenses or exposes missing/held source frontier; parallel fan-out is allowed only because the converge_on closure preserves join ordering and never reports complete on a missing source
link_movement_and_fanin_policy.reroute_budget_provenance: constitutional-default or preset-default only; NOT support-chosen (per route_policy_provenance rule)

next_building_candidate.full_run: Run work-codex (with narrow write_scope) + 3 lenses + closure once the smoke proves all 4 providers connect.
minimal_preflight_checks: adapter availability per provider: codex CLI (codex-local & codex-fugu-local/sakana routing), claude-local runtime, gemini-local CLI or gemini-api key in env
model ref resolution for model:codex:default / model:claude:inherit / model:gemini:default / model:sakana:fugu
agent_object adapter_refs admit the chosen adapter (Gemini lane MUST bind agent-object:inspector or qa)
write_scope NEED present ONLY on work-codex node; lenses+closure carry no write_scope
gate refs resolvable (link-gate:default-transition / strict) and fan-in-wait-all frontier wired to converge_on closure
next_building_candidate.smaller_read_only_smoke_first: Compose+materialize the 4-LLM graph plan WITHOUT live providers (plan-hash + topology check), then a read-only single-pass smoke: each provider runs one tiny read-only inspect over one fixed file, fan-in closure — confirms each adapter connects with NO write and NO long run.

relevant_current_structure.adapter_capabilities: codex-local & codex-fugu-local: read+write; claude-local: read+write+web; gemini-local: read+review+web; gemini-api: read+review. Only codex/codex-fugu/claude are observed-write adapters.
relevant_current_structure.agent_object_adapter_admission: agent-object adapter_refs gate which adapter a node may use. inspector = only object admitting gemini-api; qa = gemini-local; codex-fugu-local admitted by all six objects read.
relevant_current_structure.fan_graph_mechanism: graph_topology in brick/templates/presets/{triage-fanout-3,two-fan-in-graph}.md already emits explicit fan_out_groups + fan_in_groups + terminal with no positional inference; this is the proven substrate for a 4-LLM board.
relevant_current_structure.link_law: Movement literals forward|reroute only; fan-in-wait-all is a frontier observation (no GateFact literal); transition_concern_evidence is non-binding; reroute budget + closure routing are human/Link, support never synthesizes target.
relevant_current_structure.mixed_provider_precedent: brick/templates/presets/review-fleet.md already mixes providers per step (selected_adapter_ref / selected_model_ref per step) and is the closest existing template.

returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No engine/support code change needed: graph_topology fan-out/fan-in + closure_transition_target_policy already exist (triage-fanout-3, two-fan-in-graph).
No agent-object edit needed for the Gemini lane: agent-object:inspector already admits adapter:gemini-local and adapter:gemini-api.
Adapter capability table (adapter_constants.py) unchanged — all four providers already declared with capabilities.
Link contracts (movement/transition/carry/gate) unchanged — forward/reroute literals and fan-in-wait-all frontier already cover the needed semantics.
Brick return templates unchanged — work/review/code-attack-qa/axis-attack-qa/closure returns already exist for each lane.
